Can you restore a Colorbond or metal roof?
Yes. Metal roof restoration involves rust treatment, repointing fasteners, and repainting with UV-stable roof paint matched to your existing Colorbond colour.
Always. Free on-site inspections and detailed written quotes with no obligation. If you decide to proceed, great. If not, no pressure.
How long does a typical roof restoration last?
A professionally done roof restoration should last 10-15+ years with proper maintenance. We use premium-grade coatings and back our work with a 10-year workmanship guarantee.
How much does a roof restoration cost in Melbourne?

As a rough guide: a standard 3-bedroom home (200sqm) tile roof restoration ranges from $4,000 to $8,000. Metal roof restoration ranges from $3,000 to $6,000. We provide free, no-obligation quotes with a detailed breakdown.

Absolutely. A roof restoration costs a fraction of a full replacement, adds 10-15 years to your roof's life, improves energy efficiency, increases property value, and enhances curb appeal. For most Melbourne homes with aging but structurally sound roofs, it is the smartest investment you can make.
What are the signs my roof needs restoration?

Look for faded or patchy colour, moss or lichen growth, cracked or slipped tiles, rust spots on metal roofs, peeling paint, light visible through gaps, and rising energy bills. If unsure, we offer a free inspection and assessment.
What areas of Melbourne do you serve?

We cover the entire Melbourne metro area including Thomastown, Epping, Mill Park, South Morang, Moonee Ponds, Essendon, Greensborough, Coburg, Reservoir, Preston, Box Hill, and all surrounding suburbs.
What is the difference between a roof restoration and a roof replacement?

A roof restoration involves cleaning, repairing, and recoating your existing roof structure, extending its life without removing the existing roof covering. A roof replacement strips the old roof entirely and installs new roofing material. Restoration is typically 40-60% cheaper and takes 2-4 days instead of 1-2 weeks.

Why quality matters — the case for doing it properly
The difference between premium work and the standard alternative is not just the price tag. It is how it fits, how long it lasts, and how it feels.
The real difference between premium craftsmanship and standard alternatives — fit, materials, longevity, and long-term value.

If you are comparing a premium option with a standard alternative, the obvious difference is the price. The less obvious difference — and the one that matters most — is what happens a few years down the track.

## Fit

Standard options are designed to fit standard spaces. If your situation is typical and your requirements match a template, they work well enough. Most projects are not standard. Custom work is built to your actual requirements — not to a theoretical assumption.

## Materials

Explain the difference between what you use and what the budget alternatives use. Be specific — name materials, hardware brands, or techniques.

## Longevity

A well-built solution will outlast cheaper alternatives. The hardware will still perform, the finish will still look good, and the overall result ages gracefully. For most clients, that makes the premium option better value over time.

## The bottom line

Quality is not the cheapest option upfront. It is the best-value option for people who plan to stay and care about how things function day to day.

Gutter replacement, gutter guard installation, and thorough gutter cleaning to protect your home from water damage.

Blocked gutters are the #1 cause of internal water damage in Melbourne homes.

**Gutter services:**
- **Gutter replacement** — Old, rusted gutters replaced with new Colorbond steel. Available in Quad, Ogee, and Squareflute profiles.
- **Gutter guard installation** — Leaf-proof mesh that reduces cleaning.

All work complies with AS/NZS 3500.3.

Colorbond and metal roof restoration — rust treatment, repainting, resealing. Restore your metal roof's appearance and weatherproofing.

Metal roofs are durable, light, and energy-efficient. But they're not immune to rust, fading, or fastener failure. Our metal roof restoration service covers it all.

**Our process:**

1. **Inspection** — We check for rust spots, loose screws, popped fasteners, and damaged flashings.
2. **Preparation** — Surface rust is sanded or chemically treated. Loose fasteners replaced.
3. **Priming** — Anti-corrosion primer on all bare metal areas.
4. **Repainting** — Full application of UV-stable roof paint in your choice of Colorbond colours.
5. **Sealing** — All laps, flashings, and penetrations resealed.

Fully insured with a 7-year workmanship warranty.

Professional tile roof restoration — deep clean, repoint, reseal, and recoat. Restores waterproofing, lifts curb appeal, adds years to your roof's life.

Your tiled roof is built to last decades, but Melbourne's harsh sun, storms, and winter weather take their toll. Our tile roof restoration process brings it back to life.

**Our process:**

1. **Inspection & assessment** — We identify cracked, broken, or slipped tiles and check the underlying sarking and battens.
2. **Pressure cleaning** — High-pressure washing removes moss, lichen, dirt, and old coatings.
3. **Repairs & repointing** — We replace damaged tiles, re-bed ridge capping, and repoint with flexible modern mortar.
4. **Sealing & coating** — Application of premium elastomeric roof coating. UV-resistant, waterproof, and available in Colorbond-matched colours.
5. **Final inspection** — Gutters cleared, debris removed, full walk-around.

All work is fully insured and comes with our 10-year workmanship guarantee.
